question: Which kinds of instructions can a process instance modification command contain?

options.text: Instructions to activate an element of the process and to terminate an active instance of an element.
Instructions to deploy a new process definition and to undeploy the old one.
Instructions to change the type of a BPMN element in place, for example from a user task to a service task.
Instructions to add a new sequence flow between two existing elements at runtime.

a.text: The modification command can contain multiple instructions to activate an element of the process and to terminate an active instance of an element. These are the two instruction kinds documented for the command.

b.text: Deployment is a separate concern from process instance modification. Modification operates on a running instance, not on the deployed process definitions.

c.text: Modification does not allow changing the type of an existing BPMN element. It can only activate or terminate element instances.

d.text: Sequence flows are part of the process definition and cannot be added at runtime through a modification command.

explanation: A process instance modification command can carry multiple instructions of two kinds: activate an element of the process, or terminate an active instance of an element. Together these instructions let an operator effectively "move the token" within a stuck or off-track process instance to repair its state.
